steps.title: Identify the structures
steps.content: Two independent clauses surround the blank: improvements in efficiency often correlate negatively with resource conservation, though and efficiency gains, lowering the cost of use, may increase demand to the extent that resource consumption ultimately rises.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
Choice D uses conservation, though; — the comma sets off the trailing transition though, and the semicolon joins the two independent clauses.

A misplaces the semicolon before though; B and C use commas where a semicolon (or stronger punctuation) is required between the clauses.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
